content_en:
At-Tamhid is organized by the names of the narrators who appear immediately above the Companions in the chains of the Muwatta — that is, the Successors (Tabi'un) and their successors whose transmission routes Imam Malik used. For each such narrator, Ibn Abd al-Barr provides a biographical notice drawing on the biographical dictionaries of the hadith sciences, then proceeds to analyze the traditions that appear in the Muwatta through that narrator, providing extensive discussion of their chain, their meaning, and their legal implications.

The biographical dimension of At-Tamhid is one of its most significant contributions. Ibn Abd al-Barr's biographical notices for the narrators of the Muwatta are extensive and carefully researched, drawing on the full range of biographical sources available in his era. His assessments of the reliability and character of individual narrators reflect both the classical hadith criticism tradition and his own informed judgment, and they have been cited by subsequent biographical scholars as important reference points.

The linguistic dimension of the commentary is similarly developed. Ibn Abd al-Barr explains the rare vocabulary, the grammatical structures, and the contextual meanings of the traditions in the Muwatta with a command of classical Arabic that reflects deep immersion in the early Arabic textual tradition. His linguistic explanations are among the clearest and most reliable available for the Muwatta's content, and they have been cited by later lexicographers and commentators as authoritative.

The legal dimension of At-Tamhid reflects Ibn Abd al-Barr's broad engagement with all four major legal schools. Despite being himself a Maliki, he presents the positions of the Hanafi, Shafi'i, Hanbali, and Maliki schools on each legal question with remarkable fairness and precision, identifying the textual basis for each position and assessing the relative strength of their evidence. This cross-school engagement gives At-Tamhid a value for scholars of all legal traditions, not only Malikis.
content_id:
At-Tamhid disusun berdasarkan nama-nama perawi yang muncul tepat di atas para Sahabat dalam sanad-sanad Al-Muwatta — yakni para Tabiin dan penerus mereka yang digunakan Imam Malik sebagai jalur transmisi. Untuk setiap perawi seperti itu, Ibn Abd al-Barr menyediakan catatan biografi yang memanfaatkan kamus-kamus biografi ilmu hadis, kemudian melanjutkan untuk menganalisis tradisi-tradisi yang muncul dalam Al-Muwatta melalui perawi tersebut, memberikan diskusi luas tentang sanad, makna, dan implikasi hukumnya.

Dimensi biografi At-Tamhid adalah salah satu kontribusinya yang paling signifikan. Catatan-catatan biografi Ibn Abd al-Barr untuk para perawi Al-Muwatta sangat luas dan diteliti dengan cermat, memanfaatkan seluruh rangkaian sumber biografi yang tersedia di zamannya. Penilaian-penilaiannya tentang keandalan dan karakter masing-masing perawi mencerminkan baik tradisi kritik hadis klasik maupun pertimbangan independennya sendiri yang terinformasi, dan telah dikutip oleh para ulama biografi berikutnya sebagai titik referensi penting.

Dimensi linguistik komentar ini juga dikembangkan dengan baik. Ibn Abd al-Barr menjelaskan kosakata langka, struktur gramatikal, dan makna kontekstual tradisi-tradisi dalam Al-Muwatta dengan penguasaan bahasa Arab klasik yang mencerminkan pencelupan mendalam dalam tradisi teks Arab awal. Penjelasan-penjelasan linguistiknya termasuk yang paling jelas dan paling dapat diandalkan yang tersedia untuk konten Al-Muwatta, dan mereka melengkapi analisis hukum dan kritik isnad dengan cara yang membuat At-Tamhid menjadi komentar yang benar-benar komprehensif.
